definitions.definition: The flow of goods and services from a foreign country into a domestic economy.
definitions.example: India imports oil from Saudi Arabia to meet its domestic requirements.

definitions.definition: The flow of goods and services from a domestic economy to a foreign country.
definitions.example: India exports textiles to the United States.

definitions.definition: The exchange of goods and services between two or more countries.
definitions.example: International trade includes both import and export.

definitions.definition: The difference between a country's exports and imports.
definitions.example: A trade surplus occurs when a country's exports exceed its imports.

definitions.definition: The difference between a country's imports and exports, resulting in a negative trade balance.
definitions.example: A country with a trade deficit imports more goods and services than it exports.

definitions.definition: The ability of a country to produce a good or service at a lower opportunity cost than another country.
definitions.example: India has a comparative advantage in producing textiles compared to the United States.

definitions.definition: The ability of a country to produce a good or service at a lower cost than another country.
definitions.example: A country with a strong industrial base may have an absolute advantage in producing manufactured goods.

definitions.definition: A trade policy in which a country imposes no tariffs or other trade barriers on imports or exports.
definitions.example: The European Union has a free trade policy among its member states.

definitions.definition: A trade policy in which a country imposes tariffs or other trade barriers on imports to protect domestic industries.
definitions.example: A country may impose tariffs on imported goods to protect its domestic textile industry.

definitions.definition: A tax imposed on imported goods to raise revenue for the government or to protect domestic industries.
definitions.example: A country may impose a tariff on imported steel to protect its domestic steel industry.

definitions.definition: Any obstacle to international trade other than tariffs, such as quotas, standards, or subsidies.
definitions.example: A country may impose a quota on imported goods to limit their supply in the domestic market.

definitions.definition: A payment or other financial assistance given to a domestic industry to help it compete in the international market.
definitions.example: A country may provide a subsidy to its domestic farmers to help them compete in the international market.

definitions.definition: A trade agreement between multiple countries to reduce trade barriers and promote free trade.
definitions.example: The World Trade Organization (WTO) is a multilateral trade agreement.

definitions.definition: A trade agreement between countries in a specific region to reduce trade barriers and promote free trade.
definitions.example: The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) is a regional trade agreement between the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

definitions.definition: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade, a multilateral trade agreement established in 1947 to promote free trade and reduce trade barriers.
definitions.example: GATT was replaced by the World Trade Organization (WTO) in 1995.

definitions.definition: World Trade Organization, a multilateral trade agreement established in 1995 to promote free trade and reduce trade barriers.
